title: BIRDS OF A FEATHER
lyrics:
[Intro]
(But I wanna stay)

[Verse 1]
I want you to stay
'Til I'm in the grave
  > [#1, 89 votes] Billie speaks of her strong love for her partner. She expresses this intense inkling by saying that this love would last until death. This is alike the common saying: Till death do us part. This...
'Til I rot away, dead and buried
'Til I'm in the casket you carry
If you go, I'm goin' too, uh
'Cause it was always you (Alright)
And if I'm turnin' blue, please don't save me
  > [#2, 72 votes] The expression “turning blue” means someone turning sad, as the color blue is referred to as a sad color most of the time. This also can be taken literally, as someone’s skin turns blueish when they...
Nothin' left to lose without my baby
  > [#3, 24 votes] Billie feels without her lover she’s feeling reckless and a sense of apathy, hence she has “nothin' left to lose.” It can also mean the only good thing she has is her partner and if she loses them...

[Refrain]
Birds of a feather, we should stick together, I know
  > [#4, 122 votes] This is a play on the classic English saying “Birds of a feather flock together.” It is usually used to express how people with similar traits and interests gravitate towards each other. Here Eilish...
I said I'd never think I wasn't better alone
Can't change the weather, might not be forever
  > [#5, 52 votes] The common saying, “can’t change the weather” means to accept that some things are beyond one’s control. Billie uses this phrase to refer to the fact that, like her love for her partner, the weather...
But if it's forever, it's even better

[Pre-Chorus]
And I don't know what I'm cryin' for
  > [#6, 51 votes] Eillish expresses that she has such strong feelings for her partner that she is no longer in control of her emotions and they are instead in control of her. She finds herself crying out of love for...
I don't think I could love you more
It might not be long, but baby, I
  > [#7, 13 votes] Billie implies that even if there’s a possibility her life might be short and end in the near future, she still loves her partner until her death, until her last breath.

[Chorus]
I'll love you 'til the day that I die
  > [#1, see above]
'Til the day that I die
'Til the light leaves my eyes
'Til the day that I die

[Verse 2]
I want you to see, hm
How you look to me, hm
You wouldn't believe if I told ya
You would keep the compliments I throw ya
But you're so full of shit, uh
Tell me it's a bit, oh
Say you don't see it, your mind's polluted
  > [#8, 73 votes] The person she loves can’t seem to understand why she would love them. Their mind is preoccupied with all the reasons why they aren’t worth being loved. They seem to have an avoidant attachment style...
Say you wanna quit, don't be stupid

[Pre-Chorus]
And I don't know what I'm cryin' for
  > [#9, 38 votes] It can be agonizing for some people to contemplate the finiteness of everything, specially when we’ve found true love. The thought that one day the light will fade and we’ll have to bid farewell to...
I don't think I could love you more
It might not be long, but baby, I
Don't wanna say goodbye

[Chorus]
Birds of a feather, we should stick together, I know ('Til the day that I die)
I said I'd never think I wasn't better alone ('Til the light leaves my eyes)
Can't change the weather, might not be forever ('Til the day that I die)
  > [#1, see above]
But if it's forever, it's even better
  > [#5, see above]

[Post-Chorus]
I knew you in another life
  > [#10, 55 votes] These lines could be inspired by one of Billie’s favorite movies, I Origins. They may also reference Spirited Away, highlighting how Chihiro and Haku manage to remember each other despite losing...
You had that same look in your eyes
I love you, don't act so surprised
  > [#11, 64 votes] The song ends on a confirmation, if not already clear enough. that Billie loves her partner that she writes about. This is interesting when you look at its contrary song “Halley’s Comet” from her...
